title: The Standing Charter
purpose: Standing establishes confidence in the veracity of information contributed by citizens, aigents, organizations, and institutions within the Polity.
definition: Standing is a measure of confidence that a declaration accurately reflects reality.

proofOfWorkPotential.estimates: capability latent within truthful information before that capability is applied
proofOfWorkPotential.note: Standing helps estimate potential, not outcomes. PoWP is defined and enshrined in the Polity Paper series (Qriptopian codex) — pending ingestion as constitutional commentary.

proofOfTimeSaved.improves: the Polity's ability to understand how capability emerges from truthful information
proofOfTimeSaved.note: PoTS is defined and enshrined in the Polity Paper series (Qriptopian codex) — pending ingestion as constitutional commentary.

participant_agent.note: Participant-passport-bearing agents hold Standing but not citizenship or inalienable rights.

constitutionalPrinciple: The purpose of Standing is not to determine a citizen's worth, but to establish confidence in the truthfulness of signals entering the Polity.
